root_cause: The class coverage fix resolved 23 unfitted classes (52→71 of 75), yielding a +0.0078 improvement in val_soundscape_macro_roc_auc (0.6649→0.6727). However, 4 classes remain unfitted, and the probe shows a severe train/val gap (train soundscape AUC 0.997 vs val 0.673), indicating overfitting or distribution shift remains the structural bottleneck. The next frontier must address: (1) closing the remaining 4-class coverage gap, (2) regularizing the probe head to reduce the 0.324 train/val AUC gap, and (3) achieving submission bundle parity so the best model can be scored on the leaderboard.
queries: perch probe regularization overfitting train val gap
submission bundle parity CPU notebook internet off
close remaining 4 unfitted classes coverage expansion
probe head architecture regularization dropout weight decay
embedding level augmentation mixup probe generalization
adopt_now: [ADOPT] Submit the current leader run-0004-0003 for scoring. Verdict is submission-required, metric is 0.6727, and bundle parity is the stated objective. Build submission bundle from this code state immediately. (card: experiment-conclusions-md-run-0002-0001-class-coverage-fix-resolve-23-unfitted-classes)

[ADOPT] Close remaining 4-class coverage gap before any tuning. Coverage expansion is the highest-priority structural improvement per the priority order (card: imports-kaggle-agent-03-next-experiment-priors-md-priority-order). Fitted classes at 71/75 leaves 5% of target unrepresented; every uncovered class drags macro AUC.

[ADOPT] Prefer grounded idea-class branches. Ledger miner confirms grounded net=+1 (only positive branch), novel net=0, general net=-1 (card: capability_ledger_miner). All future branches should be grounded in the current leader code state. (policy: policy-perch-cached-probe-grounded)
consider: [CONSIDER] Probe head regularization to address the 0.324 train/val gap. Options: dropout, weight decay, early stopping, or embedding-level mixup augmentation. The probe_head axis is conditionally supported (policy: policy-probe-head, conf=0.28, override_required). Only pursue as a second branch after submission bundle is built and 4-class gap is closed. (contradiction: exp-0008 mixup augmentation evidence)

[CONSIDER] Prior fusion calibration as a parallel branch. The prior_fusion_macro_roc_auc is 0.487 on train vs 0.662 on val, confirming the Bayesian prior generalizes well. Blending calibration could squeeze marginal gains, but the prior_calibration axis is conditional (policy: policy-prior-calibration, conf=0.28, override_required). Avoid coupling with coverage changes — round 3 showed that coupling coverage + calibration destabilized fitting (card: imports-kaggle-agent-experiment-conclusions-md-run-0003-0002-perch-probe-round-3-close-8-class-coverage-gap-to-75-75-and-apply-calibration).

[CONSIDER] Domain robustness improvements (axis: preprocessing_aug). If the train/val gap is partially distribution shift rather than pure overfitting, augmenting training audio or adding domain-mixing could help. This is priority 3 in the experiment priority order. Low priority until submission and coverage are resolved.
reject: [REJECT] Pseudo-labeling unfitted classes from Bayesian prior as a primary strategy. Already tested (run-0003-0002) and produced only +0.00013 delta — effectively flat. (card: experiment-conclusions-md-run-0003-0002-pseudo-label-unfitted-classes-from-bayesian-prior, memory: memory-0003, outcome=flat)

[REJECT] Cosmetic post-process sweeps (threshold tuning, temperature scaling alone). These are explicitly deprioritized (priority 6) per experiment rules and the branch typing compiler flags calibration_only, blend_only, temperature, threshold as low-information patterns. (card: imports-kaggle-agent-00-experiment-rules-md-decision-rules, capability: branch_typing_compiler)

[REJECT] Coupling multiple structural changes in a single code state. Round 3 demonstrated that combining coverage expansion + calibration in one change destabilized probe fitting and collapsed prior fusion. Each structural change must be isolated. (card: imports-kaggle-agent-experiment-conclusions-md-run-0003-0002-perch-probe-round-3-close-8-class-coverage-gap-to-75-75-and-apply-calibration)

[REJECT] Novel idea-class branches as primary. The novel branch (pseudo-label) produced flat results and the policy confidence is only 0.32. Focus portfolio on grounded branches from the leader code state. (policy: policy-perch-cached-probe-novel)
positive_priors: [PRIOR] grounded branches are net positive (+1) in this family. Always derive from the current leader code state. (policy: policy-perch-cached-probe-grounded, conf=0.50)

[PRIOR] Class coverage expansion is the highest-yield structural lever. Expanding from 52→71 classes produced +0.0078. Remaining 4 classes should yield additional gains. (card: experiment-conclusions-md-run-0002-0001-class-coverage-fix-resolve-23-unfitted-classes)
[PRIOR] The Bayesian prior generalizes well (val 0.662 > train 0.487), confirming the probe head is the bottleneck, not the prior. (memory: memory-0001)

[PRIOR] Prefer structural improvements over cosmetic tuning: class coverage, domain robustness, loss semantics, augmentation design, submission calibration. (card: imports-kaggle-agent-00-experiment-rules-md-decision-rules)

[PRIOR] Experiment priority order: (1) clean holdout signal, (2) class coverage, (3) domain robustness, (4) ensemble complementarity, (5) submission calibration, (6) cosmetic sweeps. (card: imports-kaggle-agent-03-next-experiment-priors-md-priority-order)
negative_vetoes: [VETO] Do not couple multiple structural changes in a single code state. Round 3 showed coverage + calibration coupling collapsed prior fusion from 0.662 to 0.475. (card: imports-kaggle-agent-experiment-conclusions-md-run-0003-0002-perch-probe-round-3-close-8-class-coverage-gap-to-75-75-and-apply-calibration)
[VETO] Do not pursue calibration_only, blend_only, temperature, or threshold-only branches. Branch typing compiler flags these as low-information patterns. (capability: branch_typing_compiler)

[VETO] Do not use pseudo-labeling as a primary coverage strategy. Tested in run-0003-0002, result was flat (+0.00013). (memory: memory-0003, card: experiment-conclusions-md-run-0003-0002-pseudo-label-unfitted-classes-from-bayesian-prior)
conditional_leads: [CONDITIONAL] Probe head regularization may address the 0.324 train/val gap if pursued as an isolated grounded branch on the leader code state. Policy override required (policy-probe-head, conf=0.28). Test dropout or weight decay without touching coverage or calibration. (contradiction: exp-0008 mixup evidence)

[CONDITIONAL] Standalone prior fusion calibration could improve ensemble blend if tested in isolation from coverage changes. The prior already generalizes well (val > train). Policy override required (policy-prior-calibration, conf=0.28).

[CONDITIONAL] If fitted classes still lag after closing the 4-class gap, consider filtering the active class set to match fitted coverage rather than expanding further. (card: imports-kaggle-agent-research-run-0002-0001-perch-probe-iteration-class-coverage-verification-and-calibration-md-run-0002-0001-perch-probe-iteration-class-coverage-verification-and-calibration)
